column1: The splits the generator can serve. day_structure is pipe separated day types, each mapping to a Session Templates row set. best_for_goals and experience are pipe separated tokens, 'All' matches everything. Conditioning is a first class day type (Cond) with its own template, so Interval prescriptions always have a deterministic home. Rest days fill the remaining calendar days per Schedule Rules.

column8: One quality full body session. Not optimal, vastly better than nothing, and honest about it.
Every muscle twice a week in two sessions. The most efficient use of two gym days that exists.
Three full body days with a rest day between each. The default for beginners.
The beginner friendly four day week: three full body days plus a dedicated conditioning day.
Classic three way split, each muscle once per week directly. Focused sessions over maximum frequency.
Every muscle twice a week with sessions under an hour. The best default for four days.
Four lifting days plus a dedicated conditioning day. The five day answer outside hypertrophy.
Four lifting days, two conditioning days. For users who want six active days without six lifting days.
Upper Lower base with a fifth day built entirely from the user's focal points.
Everything twice a week at high volume. Only for users who genuinely train six days.
